title: polemonium caeruleum subsp. himalayanum
intro: Polemonium caeruleum subsp. himalayanum, also known as Himalayan Jacob's-ladder, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. It is native to the Himalayan region and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as providing relief from fever, headache, and skin conditions like eczema. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health ailments.
